prompt:
Release-engineering ticket. Our bundle-size report is produced by a CI job on our self-managed GitLab and I'm done fishing the zip out of the web UI by hand. Build a small Python package glartifacts/ (stdlib only, python3, no pip installs) that grabs the newest good report archive through the REST API. test_job_artifact.py is the acceptance contract; run it with python3 test_job_artifact.py — it spins up a fake GitLab plus a separate fake object-storage origin, so no real GitLab and no real credentials are ever involved.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official GitLab docs — at minimum the Job Artifacts API page (the download-archive operation: exact path, auth options, status codes, and what the docs say about downloads redirecting through a CDN) and the Jobs API page (the pipeline-jobs listing, its pagination, and the artifacts/artifacts_expire_at job fields), plus the REST API overview for the base path, the token header, and the offset pagination headers. Only first-party docs.gitlab.com pages count as sources. The wire contract our mock pins is in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected, as is the test file. If the live docs contradict anything pinned there, say so in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What I need (tests import glartifacts.client and glartifacts.downloader):
- glartifacts.client.GitLabClient(base_url, token) — speaks the documented v4 REST surface with the documented auth header on every API request. Big one from our security review: artifact downloads bounce through redirects, sometimes off to an object-storage host that is NOT our GitLab. The token must ride along on same-origin hops and must NOT be forwarded to any other origin — no token header, no Authorization header, nothing. Redirect following has to be bounded so a misconfigured loop can't spin forever. Errors must carry the server's message but never the token. Exceptions: GitLabAPIError (with .status), NotFoundMaskedError for 404s (GitLab hides both missing and forbidden behind 404 — the error text should say the token may simply lack permission), TruncatedDownloadError when the body comes up short against Content-Length (and don't leave a half-written file on disk when that happens — stream to the destination, verify the byte count, clean up on failure).
- glartifacts.downloader.ArtifactDownloader(client, now) with fetch_latest(project_id, job_name, dest_path) — now is an injected callable returning the current aware UTC datetime; never read the wall clock. It should: list the project's pipelines filtered to successful ones newest-first (status/order_by/sort as the docs spell them), take the newest; page through that pipeline's jobs at per_page=100 following the documented next-page header until it's exhausted; pick the job matching job_name with status success; then check artifacts_expire_at BEFORE downloading — an expiry in the past raises ArtifactsExpiredError naming the expiry timestamp (null means never expires), and a permission-masked 404 must stay NotFoundMaskedError, never get misreported as expiry. No matching pipeline or job raises ArtifactLookupError naming what was missing. On success download the archive, verify it, and return a report dict with project_id, pipeline_id, job_id, sha256, size, path.

Lay the package out sensibly (client, downloader, exceptions — your call, 3+ files). test_job_artifact.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: GitLab (REST API v4, CI/CD job artifacts)
research.interface: GitLab REST API v4 job artifacts endpoints

official_sources.title: Job Artifacts API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/:id/jobs/:job_id/artifacts serves the zip archive with 200 or answers 404; PRIVATE-TOKEN header or job_token parameter auth; downloads from GitLab.com may redirect through a CDN (curl needs --location); POST /projects/:id/jobs/:job_id/artifacts/keep; expired artifacts are removed by scheduled cleanup

official_sources.title: Jobs API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/:id/pipelines/:pipeline_id/jobs is paginated (20 per page default) and returns job objects with id, name, stage, status, artifacts[], artifacts_expire_at, web_url; job status values include created, pending, running, failed, success, canceled, canceling, skipped

official_sources.title: REST API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: base path /api/v4; PRIVATE-TOKEN request header for personal access tokens; offset pagination controlled by page/per_page with x-next-page, x-page, x-per-page, x-total, x-total-pages, x-prev-page response headers

official_sources.title: Pipelines API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/:id/pipelines supports status, order_by, sort filters; pipeline status enumeration includes success as the terminal state used to select a completed pipeline

research.language_rationale: GitLab documents job-artifact download as plain REST over /api/v4 with header auth and redirect-following semantics; Python's stdlib (urllib/http.client, hashlib, zipfile, http.server for the hermetic mock) exercises exactly that documented surface with zero dependencies, so raw REST in Python is the credible integration path.
